texto: it’s the way he uses language—which is nothing like the way fantasists used language before him. There’s no sense of nostalgia. There’s no medieval floridness. There’s no fairy tale condescension to the child reader. It’s very straight, and very clean—there’s no Vaseline on the lens. You see everything clearly, not with sparkles or a flowery sense of wonderment, but with very specific physical details.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Joe Fassler: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “it’s the way he uses language—which is nothing like the way fantasists used language before him. …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, fantasy, fiction pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de fiction e writing.

texto: For (Levi) Grossman, no books feel more like home than C. S. Lewis’s Chronicles of Narnia, which provide the template for what he likes to read—and how he wants to write.
